activity_1_b.text:
Listen and repeat.
සවන් දී වචන නැවත කියන්න.
கேட்டு மீண்டும் சொல்லுங்கள்.

activity_1_d.text:
Listen and repeat.
සවන් දී වාක්‍ය නැවත කියන්න.
கேட்டு மீண்டும் சொல்லுங்கள்.

activity_1_e.text:
Read along.
පිටපත සමඟ කියවන්න.
ஒரே நேரத்தில் வாசியுங்கள்.

content.sentence_text: The technician inspected the b'ea'ring in the motor to reduce noise and vibration.
The technician checked several a'i'r gaps in the motor to ensure proper cooling and smooth rotation.
Strong gl'a're from the control panel made it hard to read the indicator lights.
Excessive fl'a're from the indicator LEDs reduced visibility on the control panel.

example.text:
Listen to the model dialogues.
ආදර්ශ සංවාදවලට සවන් දෙන්න.
மாதிரி உரையாடல்களை கேளுங்கள்.

activity_2_a.text:
Match the words with the pictures.
පින්තූර සමඟ වචන ගලපන්න.
சொற்களைப் படங்களுடன் பொருத்துங்கள்.

activity_2_c.text:
Listen and repeat.
සවන් දී වාක්‍ය නැවත කියන්න.
கேட்டு மீண்டும் சொல்லுங்கள்.

activity_2_d.text:
Read along.
පිටපත සමඟ කියවන්න.
ஒரே நேரத்தில் வாசியுங்கள்.

activity_2_e.text:
Listen to each question, use the pause to answer it, then compare your response with the model answer.
සෑම ප්‍රශ්නයකටම සවන් දෙන්න, පිළිතුරු දීමට විරාමය භාවිතා කරන්න, ඉන්පසු ඔබේ පිළිතුර ආදර්ශ පිළිතුර සමඟ සසඳන්න.
ஒவ்வொரு கேள்வியையும் கவனமாகக் கேட்டு, இடைநிறுத்தத்தின் போது பதிலளிக்கவும், அதன் பிறகு உங்கள் பதிலை மாதிரிப் பதிலுடன் ஒப்பிட்டுப் பார்க்கவும்.

example_dialogues.text_a: What would you like for dessert?
example_dialogues.text_b: I’m not sure what to choose. What do you recommend?
example_dialogues.text_c: I really recommend our chocolate ice cream. Everybody says it’s delicious.
example_dialogues.text_d: OK then. Can I have a bowl of chocolate ice cream, please?

example_dialogues.text_a: What would you like for breakfast?
example_dialogues.text_b: I’m not sure what to choose. What do you recommend?
example_dialogues.text_c: I really recommend our kiribath. Many customers like it.
example_dialogues.text_d: OK then. Can I have a portion of kiribath, please?

example_dialogues.text_a: What would you like to drink?
example_dialogues.text_b: I’m not sure what to choose. What do you recommend?
example_dialogues.text_c: I really recommend our mixed fruit juice. It is very refreshing.
example_dialogues.text_d: OK then. Can I have a glass of mixed fruit juice, please?

keywords.word: a bowl of curd and honey
10 string hoppers with a portion of mutton curry

dialogues.text_a: What would you like to order for dessert?
dialogues.text_b: I’m not sure what to choose. What do you recommend?
dialogues.text_c: I really recommend our fruit salad. It’s freshly made.
dialogues.text_d: OK then. Can I have a serving of fruit salad, please?

dialogues.text_a: Would you like something for dessert now?
dialogues.text_b: I’m not sure what to choose. What do you recommend?
dialogues.text_c: I really recommend our curd and honey. We have just received a new delivery.
dialogues.text_d: OK then. Can I have a bowl of curd and honey, please?

dialogues.text_a: What would you like for your main course?
dialogues.text_b: I’m not sure what to choose. What do you recommend?
dialogues.text_c: I really recommend our hoppers. They are the talk of the town.
dialogues.text_d: OK then. Can I have five hoppers with katta sambol, please?

dialogues.text_a: What would you like for your appetizer?
dialogues.text_b: I’m not sure what to choose. What do you recommend?
dialogues.text_c: I really recommend our devilled prawns. They are out of this world.
dialogues.text_d: OK then. Can I have an order of devilled prawns, please?

dialogues.text_a: What would you like for dinner today?
dialogues.text_b: I’m not sure what to choose. What do you recommend?
dialogues.text_c: I really recommend our string hoppers with mutton curry. Everyone likes it.
dialogues.text_d: OK then. Can I have 10 string hoppers with a portion of mutton curry, please?

dialogues.text_a: What would you like for dessert?
dialogues.text_b: I’m not sure what to choose. What do you recommend?
dialogues.text_c: I really recommend our dodol. It is very popular.
dialogues.text_d: OK then. Can I have a piece of dodol, please?

activity_a.text:
Choose the correct answer.
 නිවැරදි පිළිතුර තෝරන්න.
 சரியான பதிலைத் தேர்ந்தெடுக்கவும்.

activity_b.text:
Listen and repeat.
සවන් දී වාක්‍ය නැවත කියන්න.
கேட்டு மீண்டும் சொல்லுங்கள்.

examples.sentence:
1. Can I have a box of bolts?

2. The storekeeper keeps bolts in the hardware cabinet.

content.sentence:
1. The technician brought a drum of hydraulic oil.

2. Hydraulic oil lubricates the press system.

1. The trainee carried a bundle of steel rods.

2. Steel provides strength for machine parts.

1. The supervisor inspected a roll of welding wire.

2. The welder melts welding wire during joining work.

1. We ordered two boxes of bearings.

2. Bearings help machine parts move smoothly by reducing friction.

1. The operator filled a tank with coolant.

2. Coolant removes excess heat from the machine.

1. The trainee checks lubrication before machine start-up.

2. Pass me a tube of lubricant, please.

1. The workshop received three cutting tools.

2. Cutting tools are used to shape and cut metal or wood into different forms.

1. The trainee cleaned a tray of grease.

2. Grease protects moving parts from wear.

1. We need a sheet of gasket material.

2. Gasket material prevents leakage in joints.

1. The foreman ordered four belts.

2. Belts are used in machines to transfer power between rotating parts.

activity_b.text: Can I have a box of bolts?
The storekeeper keeps bolts in the hardware cabinet.
The technician brought a drum of hydraulic oil.
Hydraulic oil lubricates the press system.
The trainee carried a bundle of steel rods.
Steel provides strength for machine parts.
The supervisor inspected a roll of welding wire.
The welder melts welding wire during joining work.
We ordered two boxes of bearings.
Bearings help machine parts move smoothly by reducing friction.
The operator filled a tank with coolant.
Coolant removes excess heat from the machine.
The trainee checks lubrication before machine start-up.
Pass me a tube of lubricant, please.
The workshop received three cutting tools.
Cutting tools are used to shape and cut metal or wood into different forms.
The trainee cleaned a tray of grease.
Grease protects moving parts from wear.
We need a sheet of gasket material.
Gasket material prevents leakage in joints.
The foreman ordered four belts.
Belts are used in machines to transfer power between rotating parts.

activity_a.text:
Arrange the following into two categories.
පහත දැක්වෙන දෑ කාණ්ඩ දෙකකට වෙන් කර සකසන්න.
பின்வருவனவற்றை இரண்டு வகைகளாகப் பிரிக்கவும்.

activity_b.text:
Listen to the audio which will be played twice and click on the correct answer. It is very important that you read the questions before you listen to the recording.
ඔබට සෑම පටිගත කිරීමක්ම දෙවරක් ඇසෙනු ඇත. නිවැරදි පිළිතුර ක්ලික් කරන්න. රෙකෝඩින් එක අහන්නට පෙර ප්‍රශ්නය කියවීම ඉතාමත් වැදගත්ය.
ஒவ்வொரு ஒலிப்பதிவையும் நீங்கள் இருமுறை கேட்கலாம். கேட்ட எண்களை குறித்து எழுதுங்கள். பதிவைக் கேட்பதற்கு முன் கேள்விகளைப் படிப்பது மிகவும் முக்கியம்.

activity_c.text:
Listen.
கேளுங்கள்.
සවන් දෙන්න.

activity_d.text:
Listen and repeat.
සවන් දී වාක්‍ය නැවත කියන්න.
கேட்டு மீண்டும் சொல்லுங்கள்.

activity_e.text: Read along. පිටපත සමඟ කියවන්න. ஒரே நேரத்தில் வாசியுங்கள்.

Questions.question: What problem from the Galle trip do the students want to avoid this time?
Questions.answer: Poor planning for transport and supplies
options: Students staying on in the bus during visits
Poor planning for transport and supplies

Questions.question: Why do they plan to leave early in the morning?
Questions.answer: To arrive before traffic becomes heavy
options: To arrive before traffic becomes heavy
Because the instructor requested an early start
To manage the long journey slower and avoid delays

Questions.question: Why does C think snacks are necessary?
Questions.answer: Lunch may be delayed and traffic could slow them down
options: Lunch may be delayed and traffic could slow them down
Students prefer snacks to proper meals
The bus company does not allow food stops

Questions.question: Why do they choose lunch packets instead of a restaurant?
Questions.answer: Ordering food is cheaper and faster for a large group
options: Restaurants near Kandy are usually crowded
Ordering food is cheaper and faster for a large group
Eating outside is more hygienic and easier for a large group.

Questions.question: What is the main reason they will ask Jim for help?
Questions.answer: He is familiar with the local area and accommodation options
options: He organised the previous trip and knows a lot about accommodation
He knows people who manage hostels
He is familiar with the local area and accommodation options

activity_c.text: So, we met the instructor yesterday, and he asked us to finalise the plan for the class trip to Kandy next week.
Last year, we went to Galle, but we didn’t organise enough transport.
This time, we need to pay more attention to buses, food, and accommodation.
Yes, I remember that trip.
We didn’t have enough water, and some students were very tired.
I suggest we book a bus early and also carry some drinking water for everybody.
We are going to travel early in the morning, so we should all be ready by 5 a.m.
That sounds like a good idea.
I agree with booking the bus early.
I also think we should arrange a few snacks, like biscuits, because there won’t be any stops on the way.
Last time, we were hungry and got stuck in traffic for more than three hours.
Right.
About food, do you think we should order lunch packets or eat at a restaurant?
In the past, ordering food saved time, but there wasn’t enough variety.
We are going to have around thirty students, so quantity is important.
I suggest we order rice and curry packets.
It’s cheaper, and everyone usually eats that.
We will also need some extra plates and spoons.
Eating at a restaurant may take too long.
Yes, that’s decided then.
We also need to think about accommodation.
We stayed in a hostel last year, and it was very noisy.
This time, we are going to stay one night near the city.
We only need a few rooms, not many.
So why don’t we talk to Jim about it?
He is from Kandy and might know some suitable places.
I can handle that this afternoon.
As for activities, we are going to visit the Temple of the Tooth and the Botanical Gardens.
Last year, we visited a museum, but some students were bored.
This plan sounds more interesting.
I agree.
We should also assign a group leader for each group.
That helped a lot on our last trip.
We don’t need much equipment, but some stationery and a printed plan for each participant might be useful.
Alright then.
I think we have enough information now.
Let’s write the final plan today and submit it to Mr Perera tomorrow.

instrction_a.text:
Watch this video to help you better understand today’s lesson.
අද පාඩම හොඳින් අවබෝධ කර ගැනීමට මෙම වීඩියෝව බලන්න.
இன்றைய பாடத்தை நன்றாக புரிந்துகொள்ள இந்த வீடியோவைப் பாருங்கள்.

instruction_b.text:
These steps will help you to learn English from this video.
මෙම වීඩියෝවෙන් ඉංග්‍රීසි ඉගෙන ගැනීමට මෙම පියවර ඔබට උපකාරී වනු ඇත.
இந்த வீடியோவிலிருந்து ஆங்கிலம் கற்க இந்த படிகள் உங்களுக்கு உதவும்.
